description: Deterministic terrain noise outputs — verifies that getTerrainHeight produces correct values from the seeded simplex noise pipeline (fBm base + valley carving + mountain chains). All heights are in meters. TERRAIN_SEED=42, 13 noise instances seeded via mulberry32.

vectors.description: Origin height: getTerrainHeight(0, 0) with TERRAIN_SEED=42. The base fBm at origin produces a deterministic value from the seeded permutation table. Mountains are suppressed within 60m of origin (spawnFade=0).

expected.note: Base fBm range is [-1,1] * TERRAIN_HEIGHT(8). Valley carving subtracts up to 6m (masked). Mountains are fully suppressed at origin (spawnFade=0 since dist=0 < 60). Exact value depends on simplex-noise library permutation from mulberry32 seeded at 42.

vectors.description: Distant point: getTerrainHeight(1000, 1000). Well outside spawn suppression zone (spawnFade=1.0). Mountain chains can add up to 45m (MOUNTAIN_HEIGHT). Height should be capable of reaching mountain zone (>20m).

expected.note: Total = base fBm (up to 8) + mountain ridge (up to 45 * ampMod) + foothill (up to 6) - valley dip (up to 5) - valley carving (up to 6). At 1000m from origin, spawnFade=1.0. If on a mountain ridge, height can exceed 20m (alpine zone threshold).

vectors.description: Shore level transition: any terrain point near WATER_LEVEL (-3.5) is at the water boundary. Terrain below -3.5m is underwater, terrain between -3.5 and -2.8 is shore zone.

expected.belowWaterLevel: terrain is clamped visually to water plane; foam generated at crossing
expected.note: Shore zone spans 0.7m vertical band. Terrain in this range gets sandy shore colour. Foam strips are generated where terrain crosses WATER_LEVEL.

vectors.description: Valley carving: stream channels carve up to VALLEY_DEPTH (6.0m) into base terrain. Ridge noise is domain-warped (warp=22) and squared (sharpness=2). A carve mask reduces carving at high base terrain values.

input.formula: carving = ridge^2 * 6.0 * carveMask, where ridge = 1 - |streamNoise(warped_coords)|, carveMask = max(0, 1 - normalizedH * 0.8)

expected.note: At ridge=1 (channel centre) with carveMask=1 (low terrain), carving is maximum 6.0m. At high terrain (normalizedH > 1.25), carveMask=0 and no carving occurs — preserving mountain summits.

vectors.description: Altitude zone boundaries: verifies the zone transition heights match the spec. Zone edges are noise-displaced by +-4m in the shader, but the base thresholds are fixed CONFIG values.

zones.name: Forest
zones.description: Full trees, grass, ferns, flowers. Green gradient ground colour.

zones.name: Subalpine
zones.description: Dark forest green. Only pine trees, grass thinning.

zones.name: Treeline
zones.description: Tan/olive tussock. Trees shrink to 30% scale (TREELINE_SCALE_MIN=0.3), then vanish at 18m.

zones.name: Alpine
zones.description: Grey-brown rock. No trees, no vegetation. Increased rock density.

zones.name: Snow
zones.description: Bright emissive white (values >1.0 for bloom). Only on flat surfaces (smoothstep(0.6, 0.85, normal.y)).

expected.shaderNoiseDisplacement: +-4m per-pixel value noise for ragged zone edges

vectors.description: Chunk boundary seamlessness: height at chunk boundary (32.0, 0) must be identical whether computed as part of chunk (0,0) or chunk (1,0). getTerrainHeight is a pure function of world coordinates — no chunk-local state.

expected.heightAtBoundary: getTerrainHeight(32.0, 0) produces identical result regardless of which chunk requests it
expected.mechanism: Height function is purely world-coordinate based (no chunk-local offsets). Noise functions are continuous. Adjacent chunks produce identical vertex positions at shared boundaries.
expected.normalSeamless: Boundary vertex normals use getTerrainHeight for out-of-chunk neighbours (VF-TERRAIN section 6)

vectors.description: Mountain spawn suppression: mountains are fully suppressed at origin (spawnFade=0), fully active at 100m+ (spawnFade=1), and linearly ramped between 60-100m.

expected.note: Ensures player always starts in a forest clearing at origin, not embedded in a mountain.

vectors.description: fBm normalisation: the fractalNoise function normalises output to [-1, 1] by dividing by the sum of amplitudes across all octaves.

expected.note: Sum of amplitudes: 1 + 0.45 + 0.2025 + 0.091125 = 1.743625. Each octave samples at frequency scale * lacunarity^i. Output divided by sum, then multiplied by TERRAIN_HEIGHT.
